topic: Short label for what is in conflict.
why_it_matters: Why this disagreement affects the decision.

resolution.rationale: How the tension was settled or narrowed. A resolved/partially_resolved status is only credited toward the contradiction score when basis != none AND it cites at least one E-### or M-###.

next_question: The question that would move this forward.
decisive_missing_evidence: Primary source, full-text passage, benchmark, or head-to-head comparison needed.

resolution_plan.proposed_experiment_or_source: What would be done — the experiment/comparison that would settle it.
resolution_plan.data_source: Where the evidence would come from — a named suite, dataset, filing, or expert pool.
linked_options: Option label (free text; matched by name)
resolution_plan._note: OPTIONAL, additive. Attach to any contradiction NOT `resolved` — a structured evidence-acquisition plan, sibling of `resolution` (never nested inside it). `evidence_type_needed` is free text (non-empty). At least one of `proposed_experiment_or_source`/`data_source` must be present. `approx_effort`/`decision_impact` are ORDINAL enums — no hours, currency, probabilities, or expected-value (EVSI/EVPI) numbers anywhere. `decision_impact: would_flip` requires a linked claim ranked `pivotal` in decision_criticality.json (07c). `linked_claims` ⊆ this record's `claim_ids`. `linked_tripwires` point at Phase 8's decision_tripwires.json when it exists. `status` is bookkeeping only — `done` NEVER implies the contradiction became `resolved`.

decision_criticality._note: OPTIONAL, additive. Set ONLY when decision_criticality.json ranked this contradiction; copied verbatim from that ranking. Ordinal-only (no numeric score). pivotal REQUIRES flips_recommendation:true. Omit when unranked.

_schema_notes: `id` is canonical; `conflict_id`/`contradiction_id` are deprecated aliases kept for one release. `resolution` is optional; when absent the contradiction is treated as basis:none (uncredited).
